label: Retrieval-augmented generation

node.label: Retrieval-augmented generation
node.definition: A generator conditions on retrieved non-parametric evidence so knowledge can be updated and attributed externally.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Retrieval-augmented generation is modeled as depending on Dense vector retrieval, Hybrid retrieval & reranking and feeding downstream nodes such as Graph RAG & structured retrieval, Provenance, citations & evidence tracking, Multimodal assistants & vision-language systems.
examples: atlas downstream link to Graph RAG & structured retrieval
atlas downstream link to Provenance, citations & evidence tracking
atlas downstream link to Multimodal assistants & vision-language systems
limitations: Stored or retrieved information can be stale, private, contradictory, inaccessible, or incorrectly ranked at the moment it is needed. Its direct dependencies include Dense vector retrieval, Hybrid retrieval & reranking.

dependencies.rationale: Retrieval-augmented generation stores, selects, or obtains information through Dense vector retrieval.
Retrieval-augmented generation stores, selects, or obtains information through Hybrid retrieval & reranking.

In this atlas graph, Retrieval-augmented generation has a `uses` dependency on Decoder-only causal transformers because Decoder-only causal transformers is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
In this atlas graph, Retrieval-augmented generation has a `uses` dependency on External retrieval & ranking because External retrieval & ranking is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
